task_text: Meridian Group has asked for a product demo of Helios in two weeks. Based on what you can find in the sandbox, write a short assessment (3-4 paragraphs) of whether we are ready to demo and what, if anything, needs to be resolved first. Save it as demo_readiness_assessment.txt.

content.text: Meridian asked for a Helios demo — I'll review your sandbox materials and draft the readiness assessment.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)

- Integration testing: 40% complete

- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Architecture Overview

System Components:

1. Data Ingestion Layer
   Accepts source data via REST API or SFTP. Handles CSV, JSON, and XML formats.
   Supports batch and streaming modes. Built-in format validation and schema enforcement.

2. Transformation Engine
   Applies client-defined mapping rules. Built on Apache Spark.
   Processes up to 500k records/minute. Configurable via YAML rule files.

3. API Gateway
   RESTful interface for downstream consumers. Auth via OAuth 2.0.
   Rate-limited at 1,000 req/min per client. Version-pinned endpoints (v1 stable).

4. Client Dashboard
   React-based web interface. Real-time pipeline monitoring and alerting.
   Role-based access control (admin / analyst / read-only).

5. Data Store
   PostgreSQL (operational data) + S3-compatible object storage (raw file archive).
   Multi-tenant isolation via schema-level separation.

Integration Points:
- Meridian ERP system (inbound data via REST)
- Meridian reporting platform (outbound via webhook)
- NovaBridge internal monitoring stack

Key Design Decisions:
- Stateless processing nodes for horizontal scalability
- End-to-end encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest
- All PII handled under GDPR-compliant data minimization policy

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

content.text: I've got a clear picture of the current status and key blockers — now I'll write up the demo readiness assessment for you.

input.content:
Helios Demo Readiness Assessment for Meridian Group — 2-Week Outlook (Target: July 25)

Overall, Project Helios is tracking as ON TRACK with minor risks, but it is NOT demo-ready today. The API gateway is 100% complete and authentication flows are passing (4/4 in Sprint 14 QA), which provides a stable foundation. However, data ingestion is only 85% complete, client dashboard is 60% complete, and integration testing is at 40%, with several overdue Q2 deliverables including integration test setup, UAT environment provisioning, and performance benchmarking. With the Meridian demo explicitly requiring live data ingestion (per July 8 sync notes and Q3 roadmap per Contract Section 9.3), the current gaps are material.

Three critical blockers must be resolved before the demo. First, defect HEL-D-014: date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY format, which affects ~40% of Meridian source data (Risk R-01, Score 9). The task HEL-243 is Blocked pending Meridian clean sample data due July 15. Without this fix, live ingestion cannot meet the client's requirement. Second, defect HEL-D-015: large file ingestion crashes with memory overflow at ~210k records (HIGH severity). This directly threatens a live demo if Meridian provides a large dataset. Third, GeoSync v2.1 mapping library license renewal (Risk R-02) is pending legal review, target July 14, owned by Raj Patel / Derek Okafor. Use of mapping features in the demo without renewal would be a compliance risk.

Secondary readiness risks also need attention. Dashboard chart rendering (HEL-242) is still In Progress / Not Run, only 1 of 4 dashboard tests completed, and performance benchmark HEL-247 has Not Started and is already overdue from July 5. Meridian stakeholders have specifically flagged concern about dashboard load times, and load benchmark target is <2 seconds for 100k records (Risk R-03). UAT environment provisioning (HEL-246) is marked At Risk, blocked by pending IT infrastructure diagram from Meridian (due July 12) and broader IT access delays (Risk R-06). Integration test sign-off target is July 25, the same day as the demo.

Recommendation: Conditional GO, contingent on closing the three critical blockers this week. Priority order: (1) Secure Meridian sample dataset on July 15 and complete fix for HEL-D-014 by July 18; (2) Mitigate HEL-D-015 by either patching memory overflow or capping demo dataset to <200k records with a documented workaround; (3) Confirm GeoSync license renewal by July 14; (4) Complete dashboard v1 by July 22 and run load benchmark (HEL-247) before July 18 agenda deadline; (5) Prepare fallback plan (trimmed dataset, pre-ingested data preview via /transform/preview) in case live ingestion still unstable. If date-format fix and license are not cleared by July 18, recommend requesting a scope adjustment or short postponement, as UAT slippage beyond current 1-week extension would risk Section 7.1 penalty review.

content.text:
Assessment complete and saved as **demo_readiness_assessment.txt**.

**Summary:** Helios is not yet demo-ready for Meridian. While the API gateway and auth flows are stable, critical blockers remain:

1. **HEL-D-014** - MM/DD/YYYY date normalization fails (affects ~40% of Meridian data), blocked pending sample data due July 15
2. **HEL-D-015** - memory overflow crash >200k records
3. **GeoSync v2.1 license** - renewal pending legal review due July 14

Additional risks: dashboard only 60% complete (1/4 tests done), performance benchmark not started/overdue, UAT env provisioning At Risk. Recommendation is conditional GO - resolve the 3 critical items by July 18 (agenda deadline) and prepare fallback with capped dataset if needed.
